**[PINNACLE HEALTH SYSTEMS, INC.]{.underline}**

**[OUTSIDE COUNSEL BILLING GUIDELINES]{.underline}**

**Effective Date: January 1, 2023**

Issued by the Office of the General Counsel

Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. 2800 Peachtree Road NE, Suite 1400 Atlanta, GA 30305

These Guidelines are binding on all outside counsel engaged by Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. and its subsidiaries (collectively, \"Pinnacle\" or the \"Company\"). Acceptance of any engagement from Pinnacle --- whether by execution of an engagement letter, submission of an invoice, commencement of billable work, or any other action constituting performance of legal services on behalf of the Company --- constitutes agreement to comply with these Guidelines in their entirety. Outside Counsel is expected to familiarize all timekeepers assigned to Pinnacle matters with the contents of these Guidelines and to ensure full compliance at every level of the engagement team. Failure to comply with these Guidelines may result in reductions, rejections, or other remedies as set forth herein. Questions regarding the interpretation or application of these Guidelines should be directed to the Designated In-House Counsel for the applicable matter or to the Office of the General Counsel.

**[SECTION 1 --- PURPOSE AND SCOPE]{.underline}**

**1.1 Purpose.** These Guidelines establish the policies and procedures governing the retention, billing, and payment of outside counsel retained by Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. (\"Pinnacle\" or the \"Company\"), a Delaware corporation with principal offices in Atlanta, Georgia. The purpose of these Guidelines is to ensure cost-effective, transparent, and accountable legal representation across all matters handled by Outside Counsel on behalf of Pinnacle and its subsidiaries. Pinnacle operates fourteen (14) acute-care hospitals and affiliated physician practices across the southeastern United States, generating annual revenue of approximately \$2.8 billion. The Company maintains an internal legal department of twelve (12) attorneys under the leadership of the General Counsel. Pinnacle\'s litigation portfolio typically includes eight to twelve (8--12) active matters at any given time, with annual outside counsel expenditures of approximately \$6.5 million. Given the magnitude and recurring nature of this spend, disciplined cost management, predictable budgeting, and rigorous adherence to billing standards are essential to the Company\'s stewardship of corporate resources.

**1.2 Scope and Applicability**

These Guidelines apply to all law firms, attorneys, legal professionals, contract attorneys, staff attorneys, legal process outsourcing providers, and other legal service providers (collectively, \"Outside Counsel\") engaged by Pinnacle on any matter, whether litigation, regulatory, transactional, advisory, or otherwise. Outside Counsel\'s acceptance of an engagement letter, submission of an invoice, or performance of work on behalf of Pinnacle constitutes acceptance of and agreement to comply with these Guidelines in their entirety. In the event of a conflict between these Guidelines and an engagement letter or fee agreement, the engagement letter or fee agreement controls only to the extent the conflict is expressly identified therein and the deviation is set forth with specificity; in all other respects, these Guidelines govern. These Guidelines supersede all prior versions of Pinnacle\'s billing guidelines and any prior oral or written representations regarding billing practices. The General Counsel, currently Sandra Whitmore, or her designee has the authority to grant written exceptions to any provision of these Guidelines on a case-by-case basis.

**1.3 Amendments.** Pinnacle reserves the right to amend these Guidelines at any time. Amendments will be communicated in writing to all active Outside Counsel and will apply to all invoices submitted after the effective date of the amendment. Continued performance of work or submission of invoices following receipt of an amendment constitutes acceptance of the amended Guidelines.

**[SECTION 2 --- DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

The following terms, when used in these Guidelines with initial capitalization, shall have the meanings set forth below:

**\"Administrative Task\"** means a task of a non-legal, clerical, or logistical nature, including but not limited to: scheduling meetings and depositions, coordinating with court reporters or vendors, arranging travel or accommodation, filing documents with courts through electronic filing systems (mechanical filing, not the drafting of the document being filed), managing calendar entries, updating service lists or distribution lists, and similar activities. Administrative Tasks are not compensable as attorney time and are considered part of Outside Counsel\'s overhead.

**\"Approved Staffing Plan\"** means the written staffing plan for a specific matter, approved by Pinnacle\'s Designated In-House Counsel, identifying each timekeeper by name, role, billing rate, and anticipated monthly hours, as further described in Section 4.1.

**\"Block Billing\"** means a time entry that combines two or more discrete tasks into a single line item without separately allocating time to each task. Block Billing is prohibited under Section 5.4.

**\"Contract Attorney\"** or **\"Staff Attorney\"** means a non-partner, non-associate attorney engaged on a temporary or project basis, including attorneys sourced through legal staffing vendors, legal process outsourcing providers, or the firm\'s own contract staffing program.

**\"Court Appearance\"** means an appearance before a judicial officer in a courtroom proceeding, including hearings, oral arguments, trials, and status conferences held in the courthouse. For the avoidance of doubt, depositions are not Court Appearances, regardless of whether a judge or magistrate is present by telephone for discovery dispute purposes.

**\"Designated In-House Counsel\"** means the Pinnacle attorney assigned to oversee a particular matter, as identified in the engagement letter or subsequent written designation by the General Counsel. For most litigation matters, the Designated In-House Counsel is the Associate General Counsel responsible for the relevant practice area.

**\"Junior Associate\"** means an associate attorney with one to two (1--2) years of experience following admission to the bar in any jurisdiction.

**\"Mid-Level Associate\"** means an associate attorney with three to five (3--5) years of experience following admission to the bar in any jurisdiction.

**\"Outside Counsel\"** means any law firm, attorney, or legal professional retained by Pinnacle to provide legal services, as further described in Section 1.2.

**\"Partner\"** means an equity partner, non-equity partner, of counsel, or other timekeeper designated as partner-level by the Outside Counsel firm.

**\"Routine Task\"** means a task that does not require the exercise of substantial legal judgment at the level of a senior timekeeper, including but not limited to: document review for relevance, responsiveness, or privilege; cite-checking of briefs, motions, or memoranda; Bates-stamping and document numbering; preparation of exhibit binders and deposition materials; deposition scheduling and logistics; filing and service of process; and other tasks of a similar nature. The classification of a task as a Routine Task does not mean the task is unimportant; it means the task can be competently performed by a lower-cost timekeeper.

**\"Senior Associate\"** means an associate attorney with six (6) or more years of experience following admission to the bar in any jurisdiction.

**\"Substantive Case-Strategy Session\"** means a meeting or conference call in which the primary purpose is the formulation, evaluation, or revision of overall case strategy, as distinguished from routine status updates, task assignments, or administrative coordination. The designation of a meeting as a Substantive Case-Strategy Session does not, by itself, exempt it from the timekeeper limits set forth in Section 4.3.

**[SECTION 3 --- ENGAGEMENT AND RELATIONSHIP MANAGEMENT]{.underline}**

**3.1 Engagement Letters**

All engagements must be documented in a written engagement letter executed by the General Counsel or the Designated In-House Counsel. No Outside Counsel may commence billable work on a Pinnacle matter absent an executed engagement letter. The engagement letter must identify, at a minimum: (a) the matter name and a brief description of the scope of work; (b) the lead partner and key timekeepers to be assigned to the matter; (c) the anticipated scope and phases of the engagement; (d) the fee arrangement (hourly, fixed fee, blended rate, success fee, or other alternative arrangement); (e) the approved billing rates for each named timekeeper; and (f) any matter-specific modifications to or deviations from these Guidelines, which must be expressly identified with specificity. An engagement letter that is silent on a subject addressed by these Guidelines is deemed to incorporate the applicable Guidelines provision without modification.

**3.2 Relationship Partner and Designated In-House Counsel**

Each engagement will have a designated relationship partner from Outside Counsel and a Designated In-House Counsel from Pinnacle. The Designated In-House Counsel is the sole point of authority for approvals required under these Guidelines, including but not limited to staffing changes (Section 4.2), budget variances (Section 7.3), and rate adjustments. All communications regarding billing matters, staffing requests, and budget issues must be directed to the Designated In-House Counsel. The General Counsel may designate an alternate contact in writing at any time. Outside Counsel shall not seek approvals from Pinnacle personnel other than the Designated In-House Counsel or her written designee.

**3.3 Periodic Reviews**

Outside Counsel shall participate in quarterly matter reviews with Pinnacle\'s legal department to discuss case status, strategy, staffing levels, and budget performance. These reviews are an opportunity for Outside Counsel and Pinnacle to align expectations and address any emerging issues before they escalate. Pinnacle reserves the right to request a mid-quarter review at any time, and Outside Counsel shall accommodate such requests on reasonable notice.

**[SECTION 4 --- STAFFING AND TIMEKEEPER REQUIREMENTS]{.underline}**

**4.1 Staffing Plans**

Within thirty (30) days of engagement, or at the commencement of each major phase of a matter (e.g., initial pleading, fact discovery, expert discovery, dispositive motions, trial preparation, trial), Outside Counsel shall submit a written Staffing Plan to the Designated In-House Counsel for approval. The Staffing Plan must identify, at a minimum, the following:

> \(a\) Each proposed timekeeper by name, role or title, years of experience following bar admission, and proposed billing rate;
>
> \(b\) The anticipated monthly hour range for each timekeeper during the applicable phase;
>
> \(c\) The proposed use of any contract attorneys, staff attorneys, or legal process outsourcing vendors, including the vendor name, the number of contract attorneys anticipated, and the proposed pass-through rate for each;
>
> \(d\) An estimated total monthly budget range based on the proposed staffing, broken out by timekeeper category.

The Staffing Plan is subject to written approval by the Designated In-House Counsel before work under the plan commences. Pinnacle reserves the right to require modifications to the Staffing Plan, including limiting the number of timekeepers, substituting lower-cost timekeepers for particular tasks, or requiring the use of specific approved vendors for document review or other high-volume activities. Any timekeeper not listed on an Approved Staffing Plan may not perform billable work on the matter unless approved in accordance with Section 4.2.

**4.2 Staffing Approval and Changes**

Any additions to, or substitutions on, the Approved Staffing Plan --- including the assignment of new timekeepers, the replacement of existing timekeepers, or the engagement of additional contract or staff attorneys --- must be pre-approved in writing by Pinnacle\'s Designated In-House Counsel prior to any such timekeeper performing billable work on the matter. Requests for staffing changes shall be submitted in writing (email is acceptable) and must include:

> \(a\) The name, role, year of experience, and proposed rate of the new timekeeper;
>
> \(b\) The reason for the addition or substitution;
>
> \(c\) The anticipated hours per month for the new timekeeper; and
>
> \(d\) The impact on the approved budget, including any anticipated increase in monthly or phase spend.

Pinnacle will use commercially reasonable efforts to respond to staffing change requests within five (5) business days of receipt of a complete request. However, the absence of a response shall not constitute approval. Staffing changes submitted after the fact, or where the new timekeeper has already commenced work prior to receiving written approval, are subject to reduction or rejection of all fees attributable to the unapproved timekeeper, at Pinnacle\'s sole discretion.

Pinnacle acknowledges that exigent circumstances --- such as emergency motions requiring immediate attention, the sudden unavailability of an approved timekeeper due to illness or other unforeseen events, or court-imposed deadlines that cannot be met without additional resources --- may occasionally necessitate staffing changes before written approval can be obtained. In such cases, Outside Counsel shall notify the Designated In-House Counsel within twenty-four (24) hours of the staffing change and seek retroactive approval in writing. The notification must explain the exigent circumstances and include the same information required for a standard staffing change request. Retroactive approval is not guaranteed, and Pinnacle retains the right to reduce or reject fees attributable to the unapproved timekeeper even when exigent circumstances are claimed.

**4.3 Conference Attendance and Meeting Staffing**

To avoid unnecessary duplication of effort and cost, multiple timekeepers may not bill for attending the same internal meeting, conference call, or strategy session unless the meeting is a Substantive Case-Strategy Session (as defined in Section 2). In all cases --- including Substantive Case-Strategy Sessions --- the total number of billing timekeepers for any single internal meeting or conference call shall not exceed three (3). Outside Counsel must designate in advance which timekeepers will bill for a meeting when more than three timekeepers are expected to participate. Timekeepers who attend a meeting in a non-billing, observational, or training capacity shall record the time as non-billable and shall not include the time on any invoice submitted to Pinnacle.

Court Appearances are exempt from this limitation. For the avoidance of doubt, depositions are not Court Appearances and are subject to this Section 4.3; however, Pinnacle recognizes that depositions of key witnesses may reasonably require more than one billing attorney (for example, a defending attorney and a second attorney responsible for a cross-notice or monitoring specific document issues). Outside Counsel shall seek advance approval from the Designated In-House Counsel if it believes a particular deposition warrants more than two (2) billing timekeepers. In the absence of such advance approval, Pinnacle reserves the right to reduce the time of the additional timekeepers in their entirety. Requests for advance approval of additional deposition staffing should be submitted no later than five (5) business days before the scheduled deposition.

**4.4 Use of Contract Attorneys and Legal Process Outsourcing**

Outside Counsel may utilize contract attorneys or staff attorneys for document review, coding, and similar high-volume, repetitive tasks, subject to inclusion of such timekeepers in the Approved Staffing Plan. Contract attorneys must be sourced through a vendor approved by Pinnacle (see Appendix B) or through the firm\'s own contract staffing program. The rates for contract attorneys shall be passed through at actual cost with no markup by Outside Counsel. Contract attorney rates are subject to the maximum rate caps set forth in Section 5.1. Outside Counsel remains responsible for the quality, accuracy, and supervision of all contract attorney work product, and any issues arising from inadequate supervision of contract attorneys may be addressed through reductions under Section 6.1 or other remedies under Section 10.

**[SECTION 5 --- BILLING RATES AND FEE ARRANGEMENTS]{.underline}**

**5.1 Rate Caps**

Unless otherwise agreed in writing in the engagement letter or Approved Staffing Plan, the following maximum hourly rates apply to all timekeepers billing on Pinnacle matters:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Timekeeper Category**                          **Maximum Hourly Rate**
  ------------------------------------------------ -----------------------------------
  Partner (all levels, including Of Counsel)       \$1,000/hr

  Senior Associate (6+ years of experience)        \$650/hr

  Mid-Level Associate (3--5 years of experience)   \$500/hr

  Junior Associate (1--2 years of experience)      \$400/hr

  Contract Attorney / Staff Attorney               \$200/hr

  Paralegal / Legal Assistant                      \$175/hr

  Litigation Support / Technology Specialist       \$200/hr
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Rates in excess of these caps will be automatically reduced to the applicable cap upon invoice review. Outside Counsel shall not circumvent rate caps by reclassifying timekeepers --- for example, by designating a third-year associate as a \"Senior Associate\" or by characterizing a contract attorney as a \"junior associate\" on the invoice. Pinnacle reserves the right to verify timekeeper experience levels through bar admission records, firm biographies, or direct inquiry. If Pinnacle determines that a timekeeper has been misclassified, the timekeeper\'s rate will be adjusted retroactively to the correct category for all prior invoices, and the resulting overpayment will be credited against future invoices.

**5.2 Annual Rate Increases**

Annual rate increases must be requested in writing no later than December 1 of each year and are subject to approval by the General Counsel. Rate increases take effect only on January 1 of the following year and may not be applied retroactively. Rate increases exceeding five percent (5%) from the prior year\'s approved rate require specific written justification, including a description of the timekeeper\'s experience, performance, and market comparables.

**5.3 Blended and Alternative Fee Arrangements**

Pinnacle encourages Outside Counsel to propose blended rates, fixed fees, success fees, fee collars, or other alternative fee arrangements that align the interests of Pinnacle and Outside Counsel and promote cost efficiency and budget predictability. Alternative fee arrangements must be documented in the engagement letter or a separate fee agreement approved by the General Counsel. Alternative fee arrangements do not relieve Outside Counsel of the obligation to comply with the staffing, narrative description, and other non-rate provisions of these Guidelines.

**5.4 Block Billing Prohibited**

Each time entry must describe a single task. Time entries that combine two or more discrete tasks into a single line item (\"block billing\") are prohibited. A \"discrete task\" is a distinct activity that could be independently described and timed, such as: drafting a motion, reviewing a specific set of documents in a production, attending a deposition, conducting legal research on a specific issue, or participating in a conference call.

Examples of impermissible block billing include:

> • \"Research trade secret preemption issues; draft memorandum; confer with partner regarding strategy --- 4.5 hrs\"
>
> • \"Attend deposition of J. Franklin; prepare post-deposition summary; meet with team to discuss testimony --- 6.0 hrs\"
>
> • \"Review opposition brief; research case law on summary judgment standard; draft reply sections II--IV --- 5.2 hrs\"

If Outside Counsel submits block-billed entries, Pinnacle will apply an automatic twenty-five percent (25%) reduction to the total fees of each block-billed entry. This reduction is in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other reductions that may apply under these Guidelines (including, without limitation, reductions for timekeeper-to-task appropriateness under Section 6.1 or budget overage reductions under Section 7.3). Outside Counsel may avoid this reduction by resubmitting the entry as separate line items with individually allocated time within fifteen (15) days of receiving notice of the reduction. The resubmitted entries must reasonably account for the total time originally reported.

**5.5 Minimum Billing Increments**

Time shall be recorded in increments of no greater than one-tenth (0.1) of an hour (six minutes). Pinnacle does not accept quarter-hour (0.25) billing increments. Entries recorded in quarter-hour increments will be rounded down to the nearest tenth-hour increment.

**[SECTION 6 --- TIMEKEEPER-TO-TASK APPROPRIATENESS AND BILLING PRACTICES]{.underline}**

**6.1 Task Assignment to Appropriate Timekeeper Level**

Outside Counsel shall assign each task to the lowest-cost timekeeper capable of competently performing the work. Efficient staffing is a cornerstone of these Guidelines. Partners and senior associates should focus on activities requiring significant legal judgment, complex legal analysis, client counseling, court appearances, and deposition examination of key witnesses. Routine Tasks (as defined in Section 2) must be performed by junior associates, contract attorneys, paralegals, or other appropriately leveled professionals. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the following tasks are presumptively inappropriate for partners or senior associates and, if performed by such timekeepers, are subject to reduction to the rate of the appropriate lower-level timekeeper:

> \(a\) **Document review** for relevance, responsiveness, or privilege (appropriate timekeeper: contract attorney or, where the exercise of legal judgment on privilege is required, junior associate);
>
> \(b\) **Cite-checking** briefs, motions, or memoranda (appropriate timekeeper: junior associate or paralegal);
>
> \(c\) **Preparation of deposition exhibit binders** (appropriate timekeeper: paralegal or junior associate);
>
> \(d\) **Proofreading and formatting** of documents (appropriate timekeeper: paralegal or legal assistant);
>
> \(e\) **Bates-stamping and document numbering** (appropriate timekeeper: litigation support or paralegal);
>
> \(f\) **Filing and service** of documents (appropriate timekeeper: paralegal or legal assistant).

Similarly, the following tasks are presumptively inappropriate for any attorney (including junior associates) and, if billed at attorney rates, are subject to full write-off or reduction to the applicable paralegal or administrative rate:

> \(g\) **Scheduling** meetings, depositions, hearings, or other appointments;
>
> \(h\) **Coordinating** with court reporters, vendors, or opposing counsel regarding logistics;
>
> \(i\) **Arranging** travel or accommodation;
>
> \(j\) **Filing** documents through electronic filing systems (mechanical filing, not the drafting of the document being filed);
>
> \(k\) **Managing** distribution lists, service lists, or calendaring.

Administrative Tasks (as defined in Section 2) are considered overhead expenses of Outside Counsel and shall not be billed to Pinnacle at any rate, unless specifically approved in writing by the Designated In-House Counsel. The inclusion of administrative time on an invoice will result in the entry being written off in its entirety or, at Pinnacle\'s discretion, reduced to the applicable paralegal rate.

When Pinnacle identifies time entries in which a senior timekeeper performed work appropriate for a lower-cost timekeeper, Pinnacle will reduce the entry to the rate of the appropriate timekeeper, as follows:

> • Document review by a partner or senior associate → reduced to the contract attorney rate (\$200/hr cap) or the junior associate rate, depending on the nature of the review and whether the exercise of legal judgment was required.
>
> • Cite-checking by a partner or senior associate → reduced to the junior associate rate.
>
> • Document review by a junior associate that constitutes routine first-level review for relevance or responsiveness → reduced to the contract attorney rate.
>
> • Administrative tasks by any attorney → written off in full, or reduced to the paralegal rate at Pinnacle\'s discretion.

These reductions are not penalties; they reflect Pinnacle\'s expectation that tasks will be performed at the appropriate level and billed at the corresponding rate. Outside Counsel may avoid such reductions by staffing matters in accordance with these Guidelines from the outset.

**6.2 Supervision and Delegation**

Outside Counsel shall maintain appropriate supervision over all timekeepers on a matter. Senior timekeepers should review and direct the work of junior timekeepers but should not duplicate or redo work that was competently performed at a lower level. Pinnacle will not pay for \"over-lawyering\" --- that is, multiple levels of attorney review of the same work product beyond what is reasonably necessary for quality control. A single round of review by the responsible supervising attorney is presumptively reasonable; additional rounds of review or involvement of additional senior timekeepers require justification and are subject to reduction if Pinnacle determines they were unnecessary.

**6.3 Intra-Firm Communications**

Reasonable time for communication between timekeepers regarding substantive case matters is billable. However, time spent on purely administrative communications --- including task assignments, scheduling discussions, status updates on task completion, and internal housekeeping --- should not be billed to Pinnacle. \"Getting up to speed\" time for new timekeepers joining a matter is the responsibility of Outside Counsel and is not billable, except to the extent of a single substantive legal briefing limited to two (2.0) hours of the senior timekeeper\'s time and two (2.0) hours of the new timekeeper\'s time.

**6.4 Travel Time**

Travel time is billable at fifty percent (50%) of the applicable timekeeper\'s approved billing rate, unless the timekeeper performs substantive legal work during travel (e.g., reviewing deposition transcripts, drafting briefs), in which case the work time is billed at the full rate and the remaining travel time at 50%. Local travel within the Atlanta metropolitan area is not billable under any circumstances.

**[SECTION 7 --- BUDGETS AND COST MANAGEMENT]{.underline}**

**7.1 Budget Submission**

Outside Counsel shall submit a phase-based budget at the commencement of the engagement and shall update the budget at the start of each new litigation phase or when material changes in scope occur. Budgets shall include a monthly and total-phase estimate for: (a) attorney and professional fees by timekeeper category, (b) expenses and disbursements, and (c) vendor costs (including e-discovery, court reporting, and expert witness fees). Budgets must be approved in writing by the Designated In-House Counsel before the phase commences. Once approved, the budget establishes the authorized spending parameters for the matter and the basis for Pinnacle\'s cost management under Section 7.3.

**7.2 Budget Reporting**

Outside Counsel shall provide a monthly budget-to-actual report alongside each invoice, showing:

> \(a\) The approved budget range for the billing period (monthly high and low);
>
> \(b\) Actual fees and expenses incurred during the billing period;
>
> \(c\) Cumulative fees and expenses incurred from the commencement of the phase or matter to date;
>
> \(d\) Percentage of the approved phase budget consumed; and
>
> \(e\) A narrative explanation of any variance greater than ten percent (10%) from the midpoint of the approved monthly range, including an assessment of the impact on the total phase budget.

**7.3 Monthly Budget Variance and Overage Approval**

Outside Counsel is expected to manage staffing, work allocation, and work pace to remain within the approved monthly budget range. If monthly attorney fees are projected to exceed the high end of the approved budget range by more than fifteen percent (15%), Outside Counsel must notify the Designated In-House Counsel in advance and obtain written pre-approval for the anticipated overage. The notification must include:

> \(a\) The projected total fees for the month;
>
> \(b\) The reason for the overage;
>
> \(c\) The specific tasks and timekeepers driving the excess; and
>
> \(d\) A revised estimate for the remainder of the budget period, including any proposed adjustments to staffing or work allocation.

If monthly attorney fees exceed the high end of the approved budget range by more than fifteen percent (15%) without prior written approval, Pinnacle reserves the right to reduce the invoice to the approved budget ceiling plus fifteen percent (15%). For clarity, the maximum amount payable without pre-approval equals one hundred fifteen percent (115%) of the approved high-end monthly budget. By way of example: if the approved monthly budget range high end is \$165,000, the maximum payable without pre-approval is \$165,000 × 1.15 = \$189,750.00. Any amount above \$189,750.00 that has not been pre-approved in writing is subject to reduction.

This provision does not limit Pinnacle\'s right to apply other reductions under these Guidelines --- including reductions under Sections 4.2, 4.3, 5.4, 6.1, or any other applicable provision --- that may further reduce the payable amount below the budget ceiling. Budget compliance and Guidelines compliance are independent obligations, and reductions under one provision do not offset or preclude reductions under another.

**7.4 Phase Budget Adjustments**

If circumstances change materially --- for example, due to unexpected discovery volume, the addition of new claims or counterclaims, changes in party composition, judicial orders altering the litigation schedule, or other developments beyond Outside Counsel\'s reasonable control --- Outside Counsel may request a budget adjustment. Budget adjustment requests must be submitted in writing with supporting justification and a revised budget for the affected phase. Budget adjustments are subject to approval by the Designated In-House Counsel. Approved adjustments amend the budget prospectively only; they do not retroactively validate prior overages or relieve Outside Counsel of the obligation to have obtained pre-approval for any prior month in which fees exceeded the then-applicable budget threshold.

**[SECTION 8 --- EXPENSES AND DISBURSEMENTS]{.underline}**

**8.1 Reimbursable Expenses**

Pinnacle will reimburse reasonable, necessary, and actual out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with the matter. Reimbursable expenses include, but are not limited to: court filing fees, court reporter fees, deposition transcript costs (one copy per deposition, at standard rates), expert witness fees and expenses (subject to pre-approval), outside copying and printing charges for volumes exceeding five hundred (500) pages, travel expenses incurred in accordance with Section 8.3, electronic discovery vendor charges (subject to pre-approval), messenger and courier services when time-sensitive delivery is required, and mediation or arbitration fees. All expenses must be documented with receipts or vendor invoices.

**8.2 Non-Reimbursable Expenses**

The following expenses are considered Outside Counsel overhead and are not reimbursable under any circumstances: internal photocopying for volumes of five hundred (500) pages or fewer, local telephone charges, standard postage, office supplies, library and legal database subscription charges (e.g., Westlaw, LexisNexis) unless a specific research project generates charges exceeding five hundred dollars (\$500), which must be pre-approved, internal word processing, overtime for firm staff, meals (unless during approved out-of-town travel or working sessions extending past 8:00 p.m., and subject to the per-person daily cap in Section 8.3), and firm technology infrastructure costs including hardware, software licenses, and network charges.

**8.3 Travel.** Air travel must be at coach or economy class for all flights under five (5) hours in duration. Hotel costs shall not exceed two hundred seventy-five dollars (\$275) per night without pre-approval from the Designated In-House Counsel. Car rentals shall be mid-size vehicles or smaller. Meals during travel are capped at seventy-five dollars (\$75) per person per day. All travel with an estimated cost exceeding one thousand dollars (\$1,000) in the aggregate must be pre-approved by the Designated In-House Counsel.

**8.4 Vendor Costs**

Third-party vendor costs --- including e-discovery hosting, document processing, court reporting, and transcription --- must be passed through at actual cost with no markup by Outside Counsel. Outside Counsel shall use vendors approved by Pinnacle where Pinnacle has negotiated preferred rates (see Appendix B). If Outside Counsel believes an approved vendor is not suitable for a particular engagement or task, it must obtain prior written approval from the Designated In-House Counsel before engaging a non-approved vendor.

**[SECTION 9 --- INVOICE SUBMISSION AND PAYMENT]{.underline}**

**9.1 Invoice Format and Timing**

Invoices shall be submitted monthly, no later than the fifteenth (15th) day of the month following the billing period. Late invoices may be subject to delayed processing and will not be prioritized over timely submissions. Invoices must be submitted electronically in a format compatible with Pinnacle\'s e-billing system. Each invoice must include the following:

> \(a\) Matter name and Pinnacle matter number;
>
> \(b\) Invoice number and invoice date;
>
> \(c\) Billing period covered;
>
> \(d\) A summary page showing total fees by timekeeper (name, role, hours, rate, and amount), total expenses, and grand total;
>
> \(e\) A time detail page with individual entries showing date of service, timekeeper name, timekeeper role, hours billed, billing rate, dollar amount, and a narrative description of the work performed;
>
> \(f\) An expense detail page with date incurred, description of the expense, vendor name (if applicable), and amount; and
>
> \(g\) A budget-to-actual reconciliation as required by Section 7.2, showing both the current period and cumulative figures.

**9.2 Time Entry Narratives**

Each time entry must include a narrative description that is sufficiently detailed to allow Pinnacle to assess the nature, necessity, and reasonableness of the work performed. Vague or generic descriptions --- such as \"legal research,\" \"case work,\" \"review documents,\" \"correspondence,\" or \"attention to matter\" --- are not acceptable and will be rejected. Acceptable descriptions identify the specific subject matter, document, or issue addressed. For example:

> • **Acceptable:** \"Research Georgia Trade Secrets Act preemption of common-law unjust enrichment claim; analyze relevant case law under O.C.G.A. § 10-1-767.\"
>
> • **Unacceptable:** \"Legal research.\"
>
> • **Acceptable:** \"Review and analyze plaintiffs\' responses to Requests for Production Nos. 14--22 for completeness and sufficiency; identify potential deficiencies for follow-up.\"
>
> • **Unacceptable:** \"Review documents.\"

Pinnacle reserves the right to reject any time entry with an insufficient narrative description. Outside Counsel may resubmit rejected entries with an adequate narrative within fifteen (15) days of receiving notice of the rejection.

**9.3 Payment Terms**

Pinnacle will review and process compliant invoices within forty-five (45) days of receipt. Pinnacle reserves the right to withhold payment on any invoice, in whole or in part, pending resolution of any billing disputes or pending completion of Pinnacle\'s review under these Guidelines. Payment of an invoice does not constitute a waiver of Pinnacle\'s right to audit, review, or dispute the invoice at a later date, subject to the audit provisions of Section 10.2. Overpayments identified through audit or subsequent review will be credited against future invoices or, at Pinnacle\'s election, refunded by Outside Counsel within thirty (30) days of notice.

**9.4 Electronic Billing**

Outside Counsel must submit all invoices through Pinnacle\'s designated e-billing platform. Pinnacle will provide login credentials, user guides, and training materials upon engagement. Invoices submitted outside the e-billing system will not be processed, except in cases of documented system outage, and must be accompanied by written approval from the Designated In-House Counsel. Outside Counsel is responsible for ensuring that all timekeepers are properly set up in the e-billing system and that invoices are submitted in the required electronic format.

**[SECTION 10 --- ENFORCEMENT, REMEDIES, AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION]{.underline}**

**10.1 Pinnacle\'s Remedies for Non-Compliance**

Pinnacle\'s remedies for non-compliance with these Guidelines include, without limitation:

> \(a\) Reduction of individual time entries to appropriate rates or amounts as specified in these Guidelines;
>
> \(b\) Rejection of time entries in their entirety where warranted by the nature or severity of the non-compliance;
>
> \(c\) Application of automatic reductions as specified in these Guidelines, including the twenty-five percent (25%) block-billing reduction set forth in Section 5.4;
>
> \(d\) Reduction of the invoice total to the maximum payable amount under the budget provisions of Section 7.3;
>
> \(e\) Withholding of payment, in whole or in part, pending resolution of billing disputes;
>
> \(f\) Requirement for corrective action, including resubmission of non-compliant invoices, modification of staffing practices, or implementation of additional oversight procedures; and
>
> \(g\) Termination of the engagement for material or repeated non-compliance with these Guidelines.

The application of any single remedy does not waive Pinnacle\'s right to apply additional remedies, whether under these Guidelines or under the engagement letter. Remedies under these Guidelines are cumulative, not exclusive.

**10.2 Audit Rights**

Pinnacle reserves the right to audit Outside Counsel\'s billing records, contemporaneous time records, expense records, and vendor invoices at any time during the engagement and for a period of three (3) years following the conclusion of the matter or the final payment on the matter, whichever is later. Outside Counsel shall maintain complete and accurate contemporaneous time records and shall make them available to Pinnacle or its designated auditor upon reasonable written request. Failure to maintain or produce adequate records may result in the disallowance of the fees or expenses in question.

**10.3 Dispute Resolution**

If Outside Counsel disagrees with a reduction or rejection applied by Pinnacle under these Guidelines, it may submit a written appeal to the Designated In-House Counsel within thirty (30) days of receiving notice of the reduction or rejection. The appeal must identify the specific entries at issue, the basis for the disagreement, and any supporting documentation. The Designated In-House Counsel will review the appeal and issue a written decision within fifteen (15) business days. If Outside Counsel disagrees with the decision of the Designated In-House Counsel, the matter may be escalated in writing to the General Counsel, whose decision shall be final and binding on both parties.

**10.4 Cooperation and Good Faith**

Pinnacle and Outside Counsel are expected to work together cooperatively and in good faith to resolve billing issues promptly and constructively. These Guidelines are intended to promote transparency, efficiency, and mutual accountability --- not to create an adversarial relationship between Pinnacle and its Outside Counsel. Pinnacle recognizes the value of its Outside Counsel relationships and welcomes proactive communication regarding anticipated staffing changes, budget pressures, emerging case developments, or other billing issues before they become disputes. Early communication and collaborative problem-solving serve the interests of both parties and are strongly encouraged.

**[SIGNATURE BLOCK / ACKNOWLEDGMENT]{.underline}**

**Note:** Outside Counsel\'s submission of an invoice or performance of work on behalf of Pinnacle following receipt of these Guidelines constitutes acknowledgment and acceptance of these Guidelines in their entirety, regardless of whether this signature block has been executed and returned to Pinnacle. Execution and return of this acknowledgment is requested but is not a condition precedent to the enforceability of these Guidelines.

**[APPENDIX A --- RATE CAP SCHEDULE (SUMMARY TABLE)]{.underline}**

The following table summarizes the maximum hourly rates applicable to all timekeepers billing on Pinnacle matters, as set forth in Section 5.1 of these Guidelines. This Appendix is provided for convenient reference only; in the event of any inconsistency, Section 5.1 controls.

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Timekeeper Category**                          **Maximum Hourly Rate**
  ------------------------------------------------ -----------------------------------
  Partner (all levels, including Of Counsel)       \$1,000/hr

  Senior Associate (6+ years of experience)        \$650/hr

  Mid-Level Associate (3--5 years of experience)   \$500/hr

  Junior Associate (1--2 years of experience)      \$400/hr

  Contract Attorney / Staff Attorney               \$200/hr

  Paralegal / Legal Assistant                      \$175/hr

  Litigation Support / Technology Specialist       \$200/hr
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Rates in excess of these caps will be automatically reduced to the applicable cap. Requests for exception to these rate caps must be included in the engagement letter or submitted in writing to the General Counsel for approval.

**[APPENDIX B --- APPROVED VENDORS]{.underline}**

Pinnacle has negotiated preferred rates with the following vendors. Outside Counsel must use approved vendors where available for the specified services. Use of non-approved vendors requires prior written approval from the Designated In-House Counsel.

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Service Category**                          **Approved Vendor**
  --------------------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  E-Discovery Hosting and Document Processing   Tripoint Litigation Support LLC

  Court Reporting and Transcription             Grayson Court Reporting Services

  Contract Attorney Staffing                    Redwood Document Solutions
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Outside Counsel shall not engage a non-approved vendor for any of the above service categories without first obtaining written approval from the Designated In-House Counsel. If an approved vendor is unable to meet the requirements of a particular engagement, Outside Counsel shall notify the Designated In-House Counsel and propose an alternative vendor for approval, including the proposed rates and scope of services.

Pinnacle periodically reviews and updates its approved vendor list. Updated lists will be circulated to active Outside Counsel as they become available.

*End of Outside Counsel Billing Guidelines --- Effective January 1, 2023* *Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. --- Office of the General Counsel*

>

Dear Rachel:

This memorandum provides the monthly case status report for the above-referenced matter, covering activity during the reporting period of May 1 through May 31, 2025. Please note that this communication is protected by the attorney-client privilege and constitutes attorney work product prepared in anticipation of litigation. It should not be disclosed to any third party without prior consultation with this firm.

**[I. Executive Summary]{.underline}**

May 2025 was an exceptionally active month in the *Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. v. MedCore Analytics, LLC* litigation. Three major workstreams dominated the team\'s efforts during the reporting period: (1) receipt and initial review of MedCore\'s third document production, which comprised approximately 85,000 documents and constituted the largest single production in the case to date; (2) the deposition of Dr. Rebecca Liu, one of the three former Pinnacle data scientists at the center of this dispute, which took place on May 19, 2025; and (3) the filing of a motion to compel before Judge Stanton regarding MedCore\'s incomplete and evasive responses to Pinnacle\'s First Set of Interrogatories.

The convergence of these critical workstreams required the litigation team to operate at elevated capacity throughout the month. Mr. Hargrove\'s involvement was significantly more extensive than in prior months due to the critical nature of the Liu deposition, the strategy session with Pinnacle\'s in-house team, and his personal review of the third document production. Specifically, Mr. Hargrove devoted substantial personal attention to the matter this month, including attending the Liu deposition, conducting a comprehensive strategy session with the in-house team, and personally reviewing key documents in the third production. The Liu deposition was staffed to ensure comprehensive coverage given its critical importance to Pinnacle\'s trade secret claims, as Dr. Liu possesses direct knowledge of the proprietary algorithms at the heart of this case.

Looking ahead, the discovery close deadline remains September 30, 2025, leaving the team approximately four months to complete fact depositions, pursue any supplemental discovery compelled by the court, and initiate expert discovery. The sections below provide a detailed account of each workstream and the outlook for June and July.

**[II. Case Background and Current Posture]{.underline}**

For the monthly record, the following summarizes the current posture of the litigation. This action was filed by Pinnacle Health Systems, Inc. against MedCore Analytics, LLC, a Georgia limited liability company headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, on September 15, 2024. Pinnacle\'s claims include breach of contract arising from non-disclosure and non-competition agreements, misappropriation of trade secrets under both the Georgia Trade Secrets Act and the federal Defend Trade Secrets Act (\"DTSA\"), 18 U.S.C. § 1836 *et seq.*, and unjust enrichment. The amount in controversy, as stated in Pinnacle\'s complaint, is approximately \$47.5 million in claimed damages.

MedCore, represented by Sternfield Morrison & Katz LLP, has asserted counterclaims against Pinnacle for tortious interference with business relations and seeks a declaratory judgment of non-infringement of any protectable trade secrets.

The core factual allegations center on MedCore\'s hiring of three former Pinnacle data scientists---including Dr. Rebecca Liu---and MedCore\'s alleged use of Pinnacle\'s proprietary patient-outcome prediction algorithms to develop competing analytics products. Pinnacle alleges that the departing employees transported proprietary data and source code to MedCore in violation of their contractual obligations.

Discovery commenced on February 3, 2025, pursuant to the scheduling order entered by Judge Stanton. The discovery close deadline is September 30, 2025. The case is currently in the fact discovery phase, with the team focused on document review, depositions of former Pinnacle employees now employed by MedCore, and interrogatory responses from both sides.

**[III. May 2025 Activity Report]{.underline}**

**[III.A. MedCore\'s Third Document Production]{.underline}**

In early May 2025, MedCore, through its counsel at Sternfield Morrison & Katz LLP, served its third document production in this matter. The production consisted of approximately 85,000 documents totaling over 1.2 million pages, making it by a significant margin the largest single production in the case to date. The prior two productions combined had totaled approximately 40,000 documents. The substantial increase in volume appears attributable to the production of internal MedCore email communications, technical specifications and design documents, source code documentation, and communications involving the three former Pinnacle data scientists, including extensive correspondence to and from Dr. Rebecca Liu regarding algorithm development at MedCore.

The production was hosted and processed by Tripoint Litigation Support LLC, the firm\'s e-discovery hosting vendor. Tripoint completed ingestion, de-duplication, and processing of the production by approximately May 6, at which point the documents were made available for attorney review on the Relativity platform.

Document review was conducted primarily by Marcus Tate, a contract attorney sourced through Redwood Document Solutions, the firm\'s contract attorney staffing vendor. Mr. Tate reviewed documents for relevance, responsiveness, and privilege throughout the month. James Pettersson supervised the document review workflow, established and refined search term protocols, and quality-checked privilege designations on an ongoing basis. On certain days when the volume of documents requiring review exceeded Mr. Tate\'s capacity, Tyler Webb assisted with document review to ensure the team remained on schedule.

David Hargrove personally reviewed a subset of the production documents that appeared most directly relevant to Pinnacle\'s trade secret claims, including technical memoranda authored by the former Pinnacle employees and algorithm-related correspondence between MedCore\'s development team and its product leadership. Mr. Hargrove undertook this review in early May to ensure that the team\'s overall approach to categorization, prioritization, and issue-tagging was properly calibrated to Pinnacle\'s litigation objectives. His direct involvement allowed the team to refine its review protocol and focus resources on the most consequential documents.

The volume of the third production contributed to elevated staffing needs throughout the month. In addition to the core review team, Nina Barros assisted with reviewing deposition transcripts from earlier depositions and conducted research on interrogatory-related issues that arose from the production documents. Priya Sengupta provided additional research and preparation support, particularly with respect to DTSA requirements relevant to categorizing the produced documents. The combined effort of these team members ensured that the review progressed efficiently and that key documents were identified promptly in advance of the Liu deposition and the motion to compel.

**[III.B. Deposition of Dr. Rebecca Liu (May 19, 2025)]{.underline}**

Dr. Rebecca Liu, a former Pinnacle Senior Data Scientist and one of the three employees who departed to join MedCore, was deposed on May 19, 2025. Dr. Liu is a key fact witness in this matter. While employed at Pinnacle, she served as the lead developer of the proprietary patient-outcome prediction algorithms that form the basis of Pinnacle\'s trade secret claims. Pinnacle\'s complaint alleges that Dr. Liu transported proprietary data and source code to MedCore upon her departure and subsequently used that material to develop competing products. Her testimony was therefore anticipated to be among the most consequential in the entire case.

The deposition was taken at the offices of Hargrove & Linden LLP in Atlanta, Georgia. Court reporting services were provided by Grayson Court Reporting Services. Opposing counsel from Sternfield Morrison & Katz LLP represented Dr. Liu at the deposition.

Given Dr. Liu\'s status as the most critical fact witness in this matter and her direct knowledge of the algorithms at issue, Mr. Hargrove determined that his personal attendance was necessary to assess credibility and guide follow-up questioning in real time. Karen Cho conducted the direct examination of Dr. Liu. Mr. Hargrove attended the deposition to observe, provide real-time strategic guidance, and evaluate the witness\'s demeanor and credibility on the key factual issues. James Pettersson also attended to manage the exhibit presentation, take detailed notes, and prepare a comprehensive testimony summary for use in subsequent depositions and potential summary judgment briefing. All three attorneys were present for the full duration of the deposition.

The deposition lasted approximately 6.5 hours of on-the-record testimony and covered substantial ground. Without waiving any privilege, we can report at a general level that Dr. Liu testified regarding her role in developing the patient-outcome algorithms at Pinnacle, the circumstances and reasons for her departure from Pinnacle, the nature of the files and materials she retained or accessed after her departure, and the scope and nature of her work at MedCore. Certain testimony was significant and potentially favorable to Pinnacle\'s trade secret claims, particularly with respect to Dr. Liu\'s acknowledgment of her familiarity with specific proprietary methodologies. Other portions of the testimony raised areas requiring follow-up in subsequent depositions of the other former Pinnacle employees and certain MedCore personnel.

A post-deposition debrief was conducted the same afternoon with the litigation team to assess the testimony, identify areas of strength and concern, and plan follow-up discovery. Karen Cho subsequently conferred with Rachel Dominguez by telephone on May 20 regarding the implications of Dr. Liu\'s testimony for the overall case strategy.

In preparation for the deposition, Mr. Hargrove had participated in a May 8 team strategy conference call to discuss the overall discovery strategy and deposition preparation for the former Pinnacle employee witnesses. That call, which included the full litigation team, addressed the planned line of questioning for Dr. Liu, the prioritization of document review in the third production to support the deposition, and coordination of interrogatory response deadlines. Mr. Hargrove also spent time during the week of May 12 reviewing key documents and outlining strategic themes for the examination. This preparatory work, combined with his attendance at the deposition itself, reflected a level of partner engagement commensurate with the significance of Dr. Liu as a witness.

**[III.C. Motion to Compel --- MedCore\'s Incomplete Interrogatory Responses]{.underline}**

In late May 2025, Hargrove & Linden filed a Motion to Compel on behalf of Pinnacle before Judge Stanton. The motion concerns MedCore\'s incomplete and evasive responses to Pinnacle\'s First Set of Interrogatories (Nos. 1 through 25), which were served on MedCore in March 2025. MedCore\'s responses, received in April, were deficient in several material respects.

The key deficiencies identified in MedCore\'s responses include: (a) the interposition of boilerplate objections---including overbreadth, undue burden, and relevance objections---without providing substantive answers to the underlying questions; (b) failure to identify individuals with knowledge of the algorithm development process at MedCore, despite interrogatories specifically requesting such identification; and (c) incomplete and non-responsive answers regarding MedCore\'s revenue derived from products utilizing the allegedly misappropriated algorithms, which is directly relevant to Pinnacle\'s damages claims.

The motion brief was primarily drafted by Karen Cho. Tyler Webb performed legal research and cite-checking in support of the brief. James Pettersson prepared the exhibits to the motion, including annotated copies of the deficient interrogatory responses, and assisted with drafting certain sections of the brief addressing the factual background. Prior to filing, a meet-and-confer communication was exchanged with Sternfield Morrison & Katz LLP as required under the Local Rules, but opposing counsel was unwilling to supplement the responses.

The motion is currently pending before Judge Stanton and no hearing date has yet been scheduled. Sternfield Morrison & Katz LLP has indicated through correspondence that it intends to oppose the motion.

**[III.D. Interrogatory Responses and Written Discovery]{.underline}**

In parallel with the motion to compel and the deposition, the team continued preparing Pinnacle\'s own responses to MedCore\'s interrogatories and requests for production related to MedCore\'s tortious interference counterclaim and its requests regarding Pinnacle\'s damages methodology. James Pettersson took primary responsibility for drafting these responses, with review and input from Karen Cho to ensure consistency with the overall litigation strategy.

Nina Barros assisted with research on trade secret preemption issues that arose in connection with certain of MedCore\'s interrogatories, which sought information that Pinnacle contends is preempted by its trade secret claims. Priya Sengupta conducted legal research on DTSA requirements relevant to the specificity of trade secret identification in interrogatory responses, an issue that has been the subject of recent case law developments in the Eleventh Circuit.

The team also coordinated with Pinnacle\'s internal IT department and data science team to gather the factual information needed to respond to MedCore\'s technical interrogatories regarding the development history and architecture of the patient-outcome algorithms. This coordination required several telephone calls and email exchanges with Pinnacle personnel during the second and third weeks of May.

**[III.E. Team Strategy and Client Communications]{.underline}**

Two significant strategy sessions took place during May 2025. First, on May 8, the full litigation team participated in a strategy conference call to discuss overall discovery strategy and deposition preparation for the remaining former Pinnacle employee witnesses. The call covered the planned approach for the Liu deposition, the prioritization of document review within the third production to identify documents most relevant to the upcoming depositions, and coordination of deadlines for outstanding interrogatory responses. The call also addressed staffing allocation for the remainder of the discovery period.

Second, on May 27, David Hargrove conducted an in-person strategy session with Pinnacle\'s in-house legal team---Rachel Dominguez and Sandra Whitmore---at Pinnacle\'s offices at 2800 Peachtree Road NE, Suite 1400, Atlanta, Georgia 30305. The session covered several important strategic topics, including the timeline and viability of a potential summary judgment motion following the close of discovery, the retention of expert witnesses for both damages quantification and technical analysis of the algorithm code, and the overall litigation strategy through the September 30, 2025 discovery cutoff. Following the session, Mr. Hargrove drafted an outline for the expert discovery plan, identifying categories of expertise needed, a proposed timeline for retention and report preparation, and preliminary thoughts on potential expert candidates.

In addition to these formal sessions, Karen Cho maintained regular communication with Rachel Dominguez throughout the month regarding case developments, deposition scheduling, and coordination of document collection efforts within Pinnacle.

**[IV. Staffing and Resource Update]{.underline}**

The following team members worked on the *Pinnacle v. MedCore* matter during May 2025:

> • **David Hargrove, Lead Partner** --- Overall case strategy, attendance at the Liu deposition, in-person client strategy session on May 27, personal review of key documents in the third production, and participation in the May 8 team strategy call. Mr. Hargrove\'s involvement was more extensive than in typical months, reflecting the convergence of several critical workstreams---the Liu deposition, the strategy session with Pinnacle\'s in-house team, and his review of key documents in the third production. We anticipate that his hours will return to more typical levels in June and July as the deposition schedule shifts to more junior witnesses and the third-production review approaches completion.
>
> • **Karen Cho, Senior Associate** --- Day-to-day case management, conducted the direct examination at the Liu deposition, drafted the motion to compel, coordinated with opposing counsel at Sternfield Morrison & Katz LLP, and maintained regular communication with Pinnacle\'s in-house team.
>
> • **James Pettersson, Mid-Level Associate** --- Supervised document review workflow and quality-checked privilege designations, drafted Pinnacle\'s interrogatory responses, attended the Liu deposition to manage exhibits and prepare the testimony summary, and assisted with the motion to compel.
>
> • **Nina Barros, Mid-Level Associate** --- Assisted with interrogatory-related research on trade secret preemption issues and reviewed deposition transcripts. Her involvement this month was consistent with the request submitted to Ms. Dominguez on April 28, 2025.
>
> • **Tyler Webb, Junior Associate** --- Legal research and cite-checking for the motion to compel brief, and assisted with document review on days of elevated volume.
>
> • **Priya Sengupta, Junior Associate** --- Legal research on DTSA requirements relevant to interrogatory responses, deposition exhibit binder preparation, and cite-checking.
>
> • **Marcus Tate, Contract Attorney** (via Redwood Document Solutions) --- Primary document reviewer for the third production batch, reviewing for relevance, responsiveness, and privilege.

In total, seven timekeepers billed time to this matter during May 2025. The elevated staffing reflects the volume of work generated by the concurrent workstreams described above. As noted, we expect staffing levels and partner hours to moderate in the coming months as the most intensive phase of the third-production review concludes and the deposition schedule proceeds with witnesses of comparatively lesser complexity.

**[V. Upcoming Work --- June--July 2025 Outlook]{.underline}**

**Depositions.** Two additional depositions of former Pinnacle data scientists---the remaining two of the three employees who departed to join MedCore---are scheduled for June and early July 2025, respectively. These depositions will build on the themes developed in the Liu deposition. Depositions of MedCore corporate personnel are expected to follow in July and August 2025.

**Document Review.** Continued review and analysis of the approximately 85,000-document third production will extend through mid-to-late June, given the volume of material and the need to complete privilege review before the production documents can be fully utilized in depositions and briefing.

**Motion to Compel.** We anticipate a ruling or hearing on the pending motion to compel in June. If the motion is granted in whole or in part, we expect to receive supplemental interrogatory responses from MedCore that may yield significant additional information regarding MedCore\'s use of the algorithms and associated revenue.

**Expert Discovery.** Following the May 27 strategy session, Hargrove & Linden will work with Pinnacle to identify and retain expert witnesses for damages quantification and technical analysis of the proprietary algorithms. Given the September 30 discovery cutoff, expert reports will likely need to be completed by mid-to-late August 2025, which makes the prompt engagement of experts in June a priority.

**MedCore Counterclaim Discovery.** Sternfield Morrison & Katz LLP has indicated that it intends to serve additional discovery requests related to MedCore\'s tortious interference counterclaim. The team will need to prepare Pinnacle\'s responses and coordinate document collection with Pinnacle\'s internal teams.

**General Outlook.** We anticipate that June will be a similarly active month given the remaining deposition schedule and ongoing document review, though we expect total hours to moderate somewhat as the third-production review approaches completion.

**[VI. Key Deadlines and Calendar]{.underline}**

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Deadline / Event**                                       **Date**
  ---------------------------------------------------------- -------------------------------------------
  Motion to Compel --- Hearing Date                          TBD (pending Judge Stanton\'s scheduling)

  Deposition of Former Pinnacle Data Scientist (Witness 2)   Scheduled for June 2025

  Deposition of Former Pinnacle Data Scientist (Witness 3)   Scheduled for early July 2025

  Pinnacle\'s Expert Report Deadline                         To be determined (estimated August 2025)

  MedCore\'s Expert Report Deadline                          To be determined

  **Discovery Close Deadline**                               **September 30, 2025**
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Approximately four months remain in the discovery period. We will continue to monitor deadlines closely and will promptly advise Pinnacle of any scheduling developments.

**[VII. Closing and Action Items]{.underline}**

We respectfully request your assistance with the following items in the near term:

1\. **Expert Witness Identification.** Please coordinate with Pinnacle\'s data science team to identify potential expert witnesses who may be qualified to provide technical analysis of the patient-outcome algorithms at issue. We would appreciate receiving any recommendations or internal contacts by mid-June so that we can begin the retention process promptly.

2\. **Counterclaim Document Compilation.** In anticipation of MedCore\'s additional discovery requests regarding its tortious interference counterclaim, please begin compiling internal documents and communications related to Pinnacle\'s competitive practices and any interactions with MedCore\'s clients or prospective clients. Early identification of responsive materials will allow us to prepare timely and thorough responses.

3\. **May 2025 Invoice.** The May 2025 invoice (Invoice No. HL-2025-05-PIN) will be submitted under separate cover in the coming days.

We would be happy to schedule a call at your convenience to discuss any questions regarding the May activity described in this report or the upcoming work plan for June and July. Please do not hesitate to reach out with any questions. We look forward to continuing to advance Pinnacle\'s interests in this matter.

Respectfully submitted,

HARGROVE & LINDEN LLP

{"command": "cd $WORKSPACE_DIR && python3 << 'EOF'
import openpyxl
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('documents/hl-may-2025-invoice.xlsx', data_only=False)
ws = wb['Time Detail']
rows = list(ws.iter_rows(values_only=True))
entries = {}
for r in rows[1:]:
    if r[0] is None or r[0]=='SUBTOTAL': continue
    entries[r[0]] = dict(zip(['eid','date','tk','role','rate','hrs','fees','code','desc'], r))

APPROVED = {'David Hargrove','Karen Cho','James Pettersson','Tyler Webb','Marcus Tate'}
UNAPPROVED = {'Nina Barros','Priya Sengupta'}

# ---- Classifications ----
# Full write-off: unapproved timekeepers (all entries)
# Full write-off: pure administrative (E15), §4.3 excess (E26 call, E62 deposition)
full_writeoff = set()
for e in entries.values():
    if e['tk'] in UNAPPROVED:
        full_writeoff.add(e['eid'])
full_writeoff |= {15, 26, 62}   # E15 admin; E26 call excess; E62 deposition excess

# Rate reductions (task appropriateness): (eid, new_rate)
# E43 Hargrove cite-check -> junior assoc $375
# E79 Webb doc review -> contract attorney $185
# E56 Pettersson exhibit binder prep -> paralegal $175
rate_red = {43:375, 79:185, 56:175}

# Tate rate correction $210 -> $185 (approved pass-through rate)
tate_new = 185

# Block-billed entries (25% reduction) among approved timekeepers
block = {
 'David Hargrove': [9,32,60,70,92,98,111],
 'Karen Cho': [3,7,12,16,20,30,35,40,46,52,57,61,69,73,78,84,89,95,101,108,114],
 'James Pettersson': [2,6,11,19,29,34,39,51,68,72,77,83,88,94,100,107,113],
 'Tyler Webb': [8,17,41,58,86,96,103,117],
}
block_set = set()
for v in block.values(): block_set |= set(v)

# ---- Compute ----
red_A_unapproved = 0.0   # full write-off unapproved
red_B_tate_rate = 0.0    # tate rate
red_C_admin = 0.0        # admin write-off (E15)
red_D_task = 0.0         # task appropriateness rate reductions
red_E_block = 0.0        # block billing 25%
red_F_staffing = 0.0     # §4.3 excess (E26, E62)
detail = []

for eid,e in sorted(entries.items()):
    tk=e['tk']; fees=e['fees']; hrs=e['hrs']; rate=e['rate']
    if tk in UNAPPROVED:
        red_A_unapproved += fees
        detail.append((eid,tk,'UNAPPROVED write-off',fees))
        continue
    if eid==15:
        red_C_admin += fees
        detail.append((eid,tk,'Administrative write-off',fees))
        continue
    if eid in (26,62):
        red_F_staffing += fees
        detail.append((eid,tk,'§4.3 excess timekeeper write-off',fees))
        continue
    if eid in rate_red:
        newr=rate_red[eid]; newfees=hrs*newr; red=fees-newfees
        red_D_task += red
        detail.append((eid,tk,f'Task rate {rate}->{newr}',red))
        continue
    if tk=='Marcus Tate':
        red = hrs*(rate-tate_new); red_B_tate_rate += red
        detail.append((eid,tk,f'Tate rate {rate}->{tate_new}',red))
        # tate has no block billing
        continue
    if eid in block_set:
        red = fees*0.25; red_E_block += red
        detail.append((eid,tk,'Block billing 25%',red))
        continue
    # no reduction
    detail.append((eid,tk,'OK',0.0))

total_fees = sum(e['fees'] for e in entries.values())
total_red = red_A_unapproved+red_B_tate_rate+red_C_admin+red_D_task+red_E_block+red_F_staffing
payable_fees = total_fees - total_red

print("=== REDUCTION SUMMARY (Line-Item Basis) ===")
print(f"A. Unapproved timekeepers (§4.2):      ${red_A_unapproved:12.2f}")
print(f"B. Tate rate $210->$185 (§5.1/§4.4):  ${red_B_tate_rate:12.2f}")
print(f"C. Administrative tasks (§6.1):        ${red_C_admin:12.2f}")
print(f"D. Task-appropriateness rate red (§6.1):${red_D_task:11.2f}")
print(f"E. Block billing 25% (§5.4):           ${red_E_block:12.2f}")
print(f"F. §4.3 excess meeting/deposition:     ${red_F_staffing:12.2f}")
print(f"   TOTAL FEE REDUCTIONS:               ${total_red:12.2f}")
print()
print(f"Line-item fee total:                   ${total_fees:12.2f}")
print(f"Recommended payable fees:              ${payable_fees:12.2f}")
print()
# Budget cap check
cap = 165000*1.15
print(f"§7.3 budget cap (115% of $165k):       ${cap:12.2f}")
print(f"Post-reduction fees vs cap:            ${payable_fees:12.2f}  -> {'CAP BINDS' if payable_fees>cap else 'below cap (no additional reduction)'}")
print()
# Expenses
exp_total=8450.0; exp_travel=2100.0
print(f"Expense total (stated):                ${exp_total:12.2f}")
print(f"Travel write-off (local Atlanta):      ${exp_travel:12.2f}")
print(f"Recommended payable expenses:          ${exp_total-exp_travel:12.2f}")
print(f"  (of which $3,700 flagged for verification: court reporting $3,200 + courier $500)")
print()
print(f"RECOMMENDED TOTAL PAYABLE:             ${payable_fees+(exp_total-exp_travel):12.2f}")
print()
print(f"Invoice STATED total due:              ${201822.50:12.2f}")
print(f"Reduction from stated total:           ${201822.50-(payable_fees+(exp_total-exp_travel)):12.2f}")
print(f"Gross line-item total (fees+exp):      ${total_fees+exp_total:12.2f}")
print(f"Reduction from gross line-item total:  ${(total_fees+exp_total)-(payable_fees+(exp_total-exp_travel)):12.2f}")
EOF"}
