**Recommendation:** apply $30,220.62. The Firm may cure by resubmitting each block-billed entry "as separate line items with individually allocated time within fifteen (15) days" (Section 5.4); several entries also bundle non-compensable administrative sub-tasks ("coordinate filing deadlines," "coordinate deposition logistics") that should be removed entirely on resubmission under Section 6.1.

### Finding 5 — Administrative Task Billed at Attorney Rate (Section 6.1) — $712.50

Entry 15 (Pettersson, 1.5 hrs, $712.50): *"Schedule depositions of three remaining witnesses; coordinate court reporter availability; confirm deposition locations."* Each component is an Administrative Task under Section 2 and presumptively non-compensable for any attorney under Section 6.1(g)–(k). Section 6.1: "[a]dministrative tasks by any attorney [are] written off in full, or reduced to the paralegal rate at Pinnacle's discretion." **Recommendation:** write off the full $712.50.

### Finding 6 — Task-to-Timekeeper Appropriateness (Section 6.1) — $3,385.00

**(a) Cite-checking by a partner — Entry 43 — $1,220.00.** Entry 43 (Hargrove, 2.0 hrs, $1,970.00): *"Review and revise cite-check memorandum prepared by T. Webb; finalize citations for motion to compel brief."* Section 6.1(b): cite-checking is appropriate for "junior associate or paralegal"; "[c]ite-checking by a partner or senior associate [is] reduced to the junior associate rate." 2.0 hrs × $375 = $750.00; reduction **$1,220.00**. (The approved junior Webb was already cite-checking this brief — Entry 47 — an over-lawyering concern under Section 6.2.)

**(b) Routine document review by a junior associate — Entry 79 — $665.00.** Entry 79 (Webb, 3.5 hrs, $1,312.50): *"Review third batch of MedCore production documents for relevance and privilege."* Routine first-level review the Plan assigns to the contract attorney (Tate was concurrently reviewing the same production with Bates-range narratives). Section 6.1: "[d]ocument review by a junior associate that constitutes routine first-level review… [is] reduced to the contract attorney rate." 3.5 × $185 = $647.50; reduction **$665.00**. The narrative is also generic and borders on Section 9.2 non-compliance.

**(c) Deposition exhibit-binder preparation by a mid-level associate — Entry 56 — $1,500.00.** Entry 56 (Pettersson, 5.0 hrs, $2,375.00): *"Prepare deposition exhibits and exhibit list…; organize documents by topic area; prepare witness examination binder."* Section 6.1(c): exhibit-binder prep is appropriate for "paralegal or junior associate." 5.0 × $175 = $875.00; reduction **$1,500.00**.

### Finding 7 — Conference and Deposition Staffing in Excess of Section 4.3 Limits — $3,312.50

**(a) May 8 team strategy call — five billing timekeepers — $937.50.** The Case Status Report states "the full litigation team participated in a strategy conference call." Five timekeepers billed it: Hargrove (Entry 22), Cho (Entry 23), Pettersson (Entry 24), Barros (Entry 25), Webb (Entry 26). Even as a Substantive Case-Strategy Session, the three-timekeeper cap applies. Barros is already written off (Finding 1); of the four remaining approved billers, one is excess. No advance designation was made, so Pinnacle retains the three core strategists and reduces the most junior excess biller, Webb. Entry 26 (2.5 hrs, $937.50) reduced in entirety. **$937.50.**

**(b) Dr. Rebecca Liu deposition (May 19) — three billing timekeepers — $2,375.00.** The Case Status Report states Hargrove, Cho, and Pettersson attended and "[a]ll three attorneys were present for the full duration of the deposition." Three billers exceeds the two-timekeeper default in Section 4.3. No advance approval was requested (due no later than May 12). Pinnacle retains the two examining/strategic attorneys (Cho, Entry 61; Hargrove, Entry 60) and reduces the additional timekeeper, Pettersson, in entirety. Entry 62 (5.0 hrs, $2,375.00) reduced in entirety. **$2,375.00.** (Pettersson's role — exhibit management, note-taking, summary — is support work Section 6.1 indicates is appropriate for a paralegal/junior associate.)

### Finding 8 — Monthly Budget Overage Without Pre-Approval (Section 7.3) — $0.00 (noted; cap subsumed)

The Plan sets a monthly range of $95,000–$165,000 and, per Section 7.3, a maximum payable without pre-approval of 115% of the high end = **$189,750.00**. Both the stated fee total ($193,372.50) and the line-item total ($215,680.00) exceed that threshold — by $3,622.50 and $25,930.00 respectively. No pre-approval was sought; the April 28 / May 1 correspondence addressed only the Barros request, and the June 1 report contains no Section 7.3 overage notification. Because the substantive reductions already bring payable fees to $140,786.88 (well below $189,750.00), the budget cap does not operate as an additional dollar reduction here, but the violation is recorded. **Recommendation:** notify the Firm in writing of the Section 7.3 failure and require an overage notification before any future month that may exceed $189,750.00.

### Finding 9 — Expense Review (Sections 8.1–8.4, 6.4) — $2,100.00 reduction; $3,700.00 flagged

**(a) Travel — $2,100.00 — non-reimbursable; full write-off.** Expense 2 (May 18, "Various," $2,100.00): *"Travel expenses for D. Hargrove and K. Cho to attend Dr. Rebecca Liu deposition — mileage, parking, meals."* The Case Status Report states the deposition "was taken at the offices of Hargrove & Linden LLP in Atlanta, Georgia." Hargrove and Cho traveled to their **own firm's office**. Section 6.4: "[l]ocal travel within the Atlanta metropolitan area is not billable under any circumstances." Section 8.2: meals non-reimbursable except during approved out-of-town travel. Section 8.3: travel >$1,000 aggregate requires pre-approval (none given). **Reduction: $2,100.00.**

**(b) Court reporting — $3,200.00 — approved vendor; verify expedited premium.** Grayson is approved (Appendix B); Section 8.1 reimburses "deposition transcript costs (one copy per deposition, at standard rates)." The "expedited delivery" descriptor suggests a premium. Require the Grayson invoice and reduce any expedited surcharge exceeding standard rates. **Flag for verification.**

**(c) E-discovery hosting — $2,650.00 — approved vendor; require vendor invoice.** Tripoint is the approved vendor identified in the Plan; amount reasonable for ~85,000 documents. Section 8.4 requires pass-through at actual cost documented by vendor invoice. **Require Tripoint invoice.**

**(d) Courier — $500.00 — verify time-sensitive justification and receipts.** Section 8.1 reimburses courier "when time-sensitive delivery is required." The motion to compel would have been e-filed (no courier needed); service on local opposing counsel could be electronic. Require receipts and justification; reduce if unsupported. **Flag for verification.**

**Recommended payable expenses: $6,350.00** (of which $3,700.00 conditional on documentation).

### Finding 10 — Over-Lawyering / Redundant Senior Review (Section 6.2) — Observation

Section 6.2: "[a] single round of review by the responsible supervising attorney is presumptively reasonable; additional rounds… require justification." The invoice shows recurring three-level review: interrogatory responses drafted by Pettersson (Entries 2, 6, 11), reviewed by Cho (Entries 3, 7, 12), then re-edited by Hargrove on May 27 (Entry 92); the motion to compel drafted by Pettersson, reviewed by Cho, reviewed again by Hargrove (Entries 32, 43); deposition outlines prepared by Pettersson, reviewed by Cho, conferenced with Hargrove. No separate dollar reduction is assigned (captured within Findings 4 and 6(a)); on resubmission the Firm should justify or remove redundant senior review.

### Finding 11 — Insufficient Time Narrative (Section 9.2) — Observation

Entry 79 (Webb): *"Review third batch of MedCore production documents for relevance and privilege"* is generic and indistinguishable from the contract attorney's routine work (compare Tate's Bates-range narratives). Reduced under Finding 6(b); any resubmission must meet Section 9.2's specificity standard.

### Finding 12 — Billing Increments (Section 5.5) — Observation (Compliant)

All 117 entries are in 0.5-hour multiples (valid 0.1-increment multiples); no quarter-hour (0.25) entries found, so no Section 5.5 rounding reduction applies. The uniform 0.5-hour granularity is coarse and may overstate short tasks; the Firm should record true 0.1-hour increments going forward. **No reduction.**

### Finding 13 — Partner Hours Exceed the Approved Monthly Range — Observation

Hargrove billed 38.5 hours against an approved range of 15–25 hours/month — 13.5 hours (54%) above the high end. The Case Status Report acknowledges the elevation and predicts a return to typical levels. Not an automatic reduction (budget impact governed by Section 7.3), but a flag: it helped drive the Section 7.3 overage and included tasks more appropriately delegated (Entries 9, 43). **No separate reduction.**

### Finding 14 — Rate-Cap Compliance for Approved Timekeepers — Observation (Compliant)

Apart from Tate (Finding 3), approved rates are within Section 5.1 caps: Hargrove $985 (cap $1,000); Cho $625 (cap $650); Pettersson $475 (cap $500); Webb $375 (cap $400). No timekeeper reclassification detected. **No reduction.**

## VI. Recommendations and Next Steps

1. **Do not pay the invoice as submitted.** Return it with the itemized reductions; require (a) reconciliation of the $22,307.50 / 61.0-hour discrepancy, (b) acceptance or contest of the reductions in writing within 30 days under Section 10.3, and (c) election to cure block-billed and task-misassigned entries by resubmission within 15 days under Sections 5.4 and 9.2.
2. **Authorize payment of no more than $147,136.88**, holding $3,700.00 (court reporting and courier) pending vendor invoices and time-sensitive justification; pay the $2,650.00 Tripoint charge upon receipt of its invoice confirming pass-through.
3. **Resolve the Barros and Sengupta staffing status in writing** under Section 4.2; until then neither may bill. Address the Firm's unilateral addition of a second junior associate with the relationship partner.
4. **Correct the contract-attorney rate** to $185/hr and obtain the Redwood invoice confirming no markup (Section 4.4).
5. **Enforce Section 4.3 prospectively** — advance designation of billing timekeepers for meetings >3 billers; deposition-staffing requests ≥5 business days ahead for >2 billers.
6. **Require a Section 7.3 overage notification** before any future month that may exceed $189,750.00; address the May overage in the next quarterly review (Section 3.3).
7. **Address the over-lawyering pattern** — enforce the single-round-of-supervisory-review presumption; reduce redundant senior review going forward.
8. **Direct true 0.1-hour timekeeping and Section 9.2-compliant narratives.**
